articles.title: Stop Blaming Herb Dean
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 15% Off with code CHAEL at https://takeultra.com #UltraPouches #ad Herb Dean has become the target of criticism after several controversial moments, but Chael believes the conversation has completely missed the point. Why don't referees ever defend themselves? Why does Herb Dean stay silent while the internet tears him apart? And are fans expecting officials to do something the rules simply don't allow? Chael explains why MMA referees operate under a different standard, why the unified rules are more complicated than most people realize, and why Herb Dean is being blamed for problems that go far beyond one official. articles.title: Dan Ige on CRAZY FIGHT vs. Diego Lopes, training with Khabib & Islam Makhachev | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by UFC featherweight star, Dan Ige, in the NEWEST EDITION of the Daniel Cormier Show! Ige talks about taking the Diego Lopes fight at UFC 303 on four hours notice, and what made him take on the challenge. Hear what happened that day, and how Ige came to the decision to fight featherweight contender Diego Lopes. Plus, Ige talks about what it was like training with UFC GOAT Khabib Nurmagomedov and Islam Makhachev, and why he was Khabib's "favorite training partner." And don't miss Dan and DC dive into his family life, his relationship with Max Holloway, Alexander Volkanovski's title reign, trying to make a name for himself when times were tough, and so much more! Timeline: 0:00 - Welcome to the show, Dan Ige! 2:00 - Parents separation & growing up in Hawaii 8:00 - How Dan started doing MMA as a kid 12:00 - Being an assistant with Ali, AKA gym 17:00 - Having a kid 19:00 - Time with Khabib Nurmagomedov 21:00 - Dan's wife Savannah 27:00 - The work to become a champion 31:00 - "50k Ige" 36:00 - Dan Ige historic fight vs. Diego Lopes 45:00 - Training with Max Holloway 46:30 - Future in the UFC and MMA All lines provided by @hardrockbet Follow me on all social media platforms! articles.title: Why Is Conor McGregor Really Coming Back?
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 15% Off with code CHAEL at https://takeultra.com #UltraPouches #ad Conor McGregor has already accomplished everything most fighters dream about. He's made the money, won the titles, and built one of the biggest brands in combat sports. So why come back now? Chael examines the biggest mystery surrounding Conor's return and explains why this challenge isn't about money, rankings, or revenge. Instead, he believes the answer reveals what made Conor one of the most unique competitors the sport has ever seen. Sometimes the biggest question isn't whether Conor can win—it's why he's willing to take the risk at all. #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #UFC #MMA #ChaelSonnen #CombatSports #FightNews #DanaWhite #UFCReturn #IrishMMABad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

Floyd Mayweather Is Broke... I Told You So!
articles.description: Use my code CHAEL to get up to 50% off your Hume Pod: https://sponsr.is/humehealth_chael #HumeHealth #smartscale #weightloss Rumors about Floyd Mayweather's financial problems have reignited one of boxing's oldest debates: were those massive payday numbers ever real in the first place? Chael breaks down why he's questioned boxing's reported purses for years, why UFC's payment structure changed the sport forever, and why helping Floyd now may not solve the real problem. He also explains why the biggest story was never how much Floyd supposedly made—but who was actually writing those checks. If Floyd really is in financial trouble, Chael believes the question isn't whether he can make more money. It's whether anything would actually change. articles.title: Jon Jones Has No One to Fight
articles.description: Use my code CHAEL to get up to 50% off your Hume Pod: https://sponsr.is/humehealth_chael #HumeHealth #smartscale #weightloss Jon Jones says he'll fight again—but who should it be against? Chael looks at the shrinking list of realistic opponents, why the Tom Aspinall fight has lost momentum, and how the heavyweight division could move on without Jon if he waits too long. He also explains why becoming champion again could actually create a bigger problem for the UFC than not having Jon fight at all. With Ciryl Gane, Tom Aspinall, Alex Pereira, Josh Hokit, and even Gable Steveson all affecting the future of the division, the clock may finally be working against the greatest fighter of all time. articles.title: Ian Garry predicted a 4th round finish vs Islam🤯 #shorts #ufc #mma #islammakhachev
Daniel Cormier WARNS Ian Garry about wanting 4TH ROUND FINISH vs. Islam Makhachev at UFC 330
articles.description: Daniel Cormier IS BACK to talk all things UFC and MMA, diving into the tension building between Ian Garry and Islam Makhachev ahead of their title fight in Philadelphia at UFC 330! DC warns Ian Garry that despite LOVING the confidence, he needs to be careful about his approach to this fight. Cormier thinks it's the wrong move to call for a fourth round finish because you want to avoid people thinking the win is "a fluke." Hear DC break down Garry's approach to the fight, and why despite not agreeing with his approach, Cormier appreciates the confidence against today's No. 1 ranked pound-for-pound fighter in the UFC in Islam. articles.title: Gaethje Refused Arman’s Gift #ufc
articles.description: Why would Justin Gaethje turn down a free truck from Arman Tsarukyan? According to Chael Sonnen, the answer tells you everything about how elite competitors think. After Tsarukyan reportedly won millions by betting on Gaethje and offered to buy him a pickup truck as a gift, many fans assumed Justin would happily accept it. Chael disagrees. In fact, he believes Gaethje's refusal reveals the mindset that helped make him UFC champion. In this breakdown, Chael jokes that he would have taken the truck immediately. Sell it, auction it, give it away—there are plenty of options. But world champions don't always think like the rest of us. Chael argues that for Gaethje, accepting a gift from a future opponent creates a problem. The first issue is pride. The second—and more important one—is competition. If Gaethje and Tsarukyan are headed toward a title fight, Justin doesn't want to feel like he owes Arman anything. Not a favor. Not gratitude. Not even a small gesture. When the cage door closes, Gaethje wants complete freedom to do whatever is necessary to win. Chael believes that decision perfectly captures the mentality of elite fighters. While most people see a free truck, champions see potential distractions. The result is one of the most fascinating stories in MMA today as the UFC moves closer to a possible Gaethje vs. Tsarukyan showdown. For more UFC analysis, Justin Gaethje news, Arman Tsarukyan updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #JustinGaethje #ArmanTsarukyan #ChaelSonnen #MMA #LightweightTitle #UFCTalk #CombatSports

articles.title: ‘Let’s Grapple!’ Rodtang & DJ Train Together
Can Justin Gaethje Beat Arman Tsarukyan? #ufc
articles.description: Can Justin Gaethje defeat Arman Tsarukyan? According to Chael Sonnen, that's the only question that really matters when discussing the UFC lightweight champion's future. In this breakdown, Chael explores the crossroads Gaethje now faces. Retirement is being discussed, but Chael isn't convinced anyone truly knows what Justin is thinking. If Gaethje believes he can beat Tsarukyan, then the path is simple: defend the title against the division's top contender. But if he doesn't believe he can win, Chael argues the decision becomes much more complicated. What makes Gaethje unique, however, is his mentality. Chael points out that two of Justin's greatest victories came in fights where many believed he was the underdog. Gaethje has built his reputation on embracing difficult challenges rather than avoiding them, which is why Chael refuses to predict what he'll do next. One thing Chael does believe is unlikely: Gaethje isn't going to spend the next few years rebuilding as a contender. At 37 years old and coming off the biggest victory of his career, the next move is likely to be either another championship fight or the end of an unforgettable run. Chael also reminds fans how often they've been wrong about Gaethje's career. Time after time, people have counted him out—and time after time, he's proven them wrong. That's why Chael says nobody should pretend to know what's coming next. For more UFC analysis, Justin Gaethje news, Arman Tsarukyan updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #JustinGaethje #ArmanTsarukyan #ChaelSonnen #MMA #LightweightTitle #UFCFightNews #CombatSports

articles.title: ‘Justin Can Retire!’ Paddy & DJ On Gaethje Retiring
Ilia Topuria Must Wait #ufc
articles.description: Ilia Topuria may have one of the strongest résumés in the UFC today, but Chael Sonnen doesn't believe he'll get an immediate rematch with Justin Gaethje. In this breakdown, Chael argues that Topuria's accomplishments already place him among the sport's elite. He even suggests that, if not for UFC timing and paperwork, Ilia could have simultaneously held the featherweight title, lightweight title, and BMF championship. Despite that, Chael thinks the UFC is unlikely to send him straight back into another title fight against Gaethje. The reason isn't that Topuria hasn't earned it—it's that Gaethje has options. After capturing the championship, Chael believes Justin has done enough to pursue fresh matchups rather than an immediate rematch. He also points out another major factor that could reshape the division: Conor McGregor. If McGregor returns, defeats Max Holloway, and follows through on his stated plan to fight twice, Chael believes a blockbuster showdown with Gaethje could become the UFC's priority. That scenario would push Topuria's title hopes further down the timeline, even if fans are eager to see him fight for gold again. According to Chael, the smartest move for Ilia is to heal, stay ready, and wait for the next championship opportunity. For more UFC analysis, Ilia Topuria updates, Justin Gaethje news, Conor McGregor coverage, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #IliaTopuria #JustinGaethje #ConorMcGregor #MaxHolloway #ChaelSonnen #MMA

articles.title: Josh Hokit’s Fast Track to the Belt  #ufc
articles.description: Chael Sonnen believes Josh Hoekstra is much closer to a UFC heavyweight title shot than most fans realize. In this breakdown, Chael explains why Hoekstra's path to championship gold may be just one fight away—or possibly even immediate. The biggest factor isn't Hoekstra himself. It's the uncertainty surrounding heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall. If Aspinall returns to defend against interim champion Ciryl Gane, Chael expects Hoekstra to stay active against another top contender while waiting for his opportunity. However, if Aspinall remains unavailable and the UFC decides not to book an immediate rematch involving Alex Pereira, Chael believes Gane could be elevated to undisputed champion. In that scenario, Hoekstra could find himself fighting for the heavyweight title much sooner than anyone expected. One thing Chael is confident about is Hoekstra's timeline. Whether his next fight is for the championship or one final eliminator, Chael doesn't see any realistic path where Hoekstra has to win multiple contender fights before competing for UFC gold. His long-term future looks exceptionally bright, and the heavyweight division may be on the verge of welcoming its next title challenger. For more UFC analysis, heavyweight news, Josh Hoekstra updates, and Chael Sonnen breakdowns, subscribe for daily MMA content. #UFC #JoshHoekstra #TomAspinall #CirylGane #AlexPereira #ChaelSonnen #MMA #HeavyweightTitle
